fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 66 is inverted. The clamping logic is executed when `clamp` is false and skipped when `clamp` is true, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: (not clamp and y__5 == y__1) or (clamp and y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After the conditional block, the value of y must be consistent with the clamp flag: unclamped if false, within the [y0, y1] range if true.
When not clamping (i.e., inside the 'if not clamp:' branch), the value of y should not be modified.

spec.expr: y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: When clamping is enabled (i.e., inside the else branch), the value of y must be within the allowed range.
The initial interpolated value y should be correctly calculated based on the parameter t.
